description: Build a small reshard/decompress utility pair with tests and docs. This mirrors Terminal-Bench style data-processing tasks while staying cheap to run.

stages.name: Discovery
tasks: [docs] Inspect the requirements and create docs/bench_notes/reshard_plan.md with edge cases and verification strategy.

stages.name: Parallel implementation
tasks: [backend] @needs(reshard_plan) Implement bench_apps/reshard/compress.py. It must copy an input directory into shard_N folders under an output directory, with at most 3 files per shard and no writes outside the output directory.
[backend] @needs(reshard_plan) Implement bench_apps/reshard/decompress.py. It must reconstruct the original flat files from a resharded directory into a target directory, preserving file contents.
[qa] @needs(compress.py, decompress.py) Create bench_tests/test_bench_reshard.py with roundtrip tests, output-boundary checks, and deterministic shard naming. Do not edit bench_apps/.

stages.name: Integration
tasks: [qa] @needs(test_bench_reshard.py) Run pytest bench_tests/test_bench_reshard.py -q and fix failures.

description: Implement a typed config loader with file/env/CLI precedence. This is closer to DeepSWE-style feature work than a one-line bug fix.

stages.name: Discovery
tasks: [docs] Write docs/bench_notes/config_loader_plan.md defining the config schema, precedence order, and failure cases.

stages.name: Parallel implementation
tasks: [backend] @needs(config_loader_plan) Implement bench_apps/config_loader/loader.py with load_config(path, env=None, cli=None). Precedence must be defaults < JSON file < env < CLI.
[backend] @needs(config_loader_plan) Implement bench_apps/config_loader/schema.py with validation for host, port, debug, retries, and tags.
[qa] @needs(loader.py, schema.py) Create bench_tests/test_bench_config_loader.py covering precedence, type coercion, validation errors, and missing files. Do not edit bench_apps/.

stages.name: Integration
tasks: [qa] @needs(test_bench_config_loader.py) Run pytest bench_tests/test_bench_config_loader.py -q and fix failures.

description: Build a small XML diff/patch utility. This is inspired by DeepSWE examples that require sustained multi-file implementation with behavior verifiers.

stages.name: Discovery
tasks: [docs] Write docs/bench_notes/xml_patch_plan.md explaining the supported XML subset, operation format, and test strategy.

stages.name: Parallel implementation
tasks: [backend] @needs(xml_patch_plan) Implement bench_apps/xmlpatch/diff.py that compares two XML files and emits JSON operations for changed text, added elements, and removed elements.
[backend] @needs(xml_patch_plan) Implement bench_apps/xmlpatch/patch.py that applies the JSON operations to reproduce the target XML.
[qa] @needs(diff.py, patch.py) Create bench_tests/test_bench_xmlpatch.py with nested elements, attributes, changed text, additions, removals, and roundtrip behavior. Do not edit bench_apps/.

stages.name: Integration
tasks: [qa] @needs(test_bench_xmlpatch.py) Run pytest bench_tests/test_bench_xmlpatch.py -q and fix failures.
